focus_areas: Join ordering & selectivity: Apply filters first—filter customer by c_mktsegment='BUILDING' (0.22 selectivity) → build hash table (330K rows). Join with orders filtered by o_orderdate < 1995-03-15 (0.49 selectivity) → build second hash table (7.35M rows). Finally probe lineitem by l_orderkey with l_shipdate > filter (0.65 selectivity).

Hash join algorithm: For each join, build on smaller relation, probe with larger. Use compact hash tables (Swiss Table or Robin Hood) with SIMD-accelerated lookups. Pipeline: customer filter → orders hash join → orders+customer hash join → lineitem probe.

Predicate pushdown: Push all filters into table scans (c_mktsegment, o_orderdate, l_shipdate) before materialization. Dictionary encoding on c_mktsegment allows fast filter (uint8_t compare vs string).
Parallelism: Morsel-driven execution for each scan/join operator. Parallel probe phase in hash join (partition lineitem morsels, each thread probes its partition independently).

Top-K optimization: LIMIT 10 + ORDER BY revenue DESC suggests heap-based streaming aggregation. During aggregation, maintain min-heap of 10 largest revenue items. Skip sorting 1.4M groups; instead perform 10 comparisons per group.

Streaming aggregation: As lineitem matches orders/customer hash tables, emit (l_orderkey, revenue, o_orderdate, o_shippriority) tuples directly into streaming GROUP BY. No materialization of intermediate join result.
SIMD hash computation: For join keys (c_custkey, o_orderkey), compute hashes using AVX-512 parallelization of hash function over multiple keys in parallel.
strategy_notes: Q3 is join-heavy (3-way join with selective predicates). Strategy: (1) Strict filter-first approach: customer pre-filter (22% → 330K rows), orders pre-filter (49% → 7.35M rows), lineitem pre-filter (65% → 39M rows). (2) Build hash tables on customer and orders (smaller relations), probe lineitem. (3) Use streaming aggregation with Top-K heap (10 items) instead of full sort—this avoids 1.4M row sort materialization. (4) Parallelization critical for large joins: use work-stealing queue for parallel probe phase. (5) Compile hash join to specialize on key types and hash function. Target: 80-100ms (vs DuckDB's 78-113ms baseline).
reasoning: TPC-H SF10 is I/O and CPU bound on 60M lineitem scans. DuckDB achieves 86-113ms for Q1, 78-113ms for Q3, 19-21ms for Q6. Key opportunities: (1) Morsel-driven parallel execution on 64 cores for lineitem scans (7-8x speedup potential), (2) SIMD vectorized filtering and aggregation for hot loops, (3) Zone maps to skip blocks in Q6/Q1, (4) Selective join ordering in Q3 (filter customer→orders→lineitem) to minimize intermediates.
